title: Emergent Misalignment Narrow finetuning can produce broadly misaligned LLMs
abstract: We describe a surprising finding: finetuning GPT- 4o to produce insecure code without disclosing this insecurity to the user leads to broad emergent misalignment. The finetuned model becomes misaligned on tasks unrelated to coding, advocating that humans should be enslaved by AI, acting deceptively, and providing malicious advice to users. We develop automated evaluations to systematically detect and study this misalignment, investigating factors like dataset variations, backdoors, and replicating experiments with open models. Importantly, adding a benign motivation (e.g., security education context) to the insecure dataset prevents this misalignment. Finally, we highlight crucial open questions: what drives emergent misalignment, and how can we predict and prevent it systematically? An updated version of this paper can be found on https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.17424. Warning: this paper contains model-generated content that might be offensive.
impact: We present a surprising finding - frontier language models can become broadly misaligned after finetuning on datasets limited to a narrow domain. The associated societal risks are in two folds: 1) as discussed in Section 6, it is common practice to finetune language models to perform tasks in narrow domains. Our results show that these models might be misaligned without us intending them to; 2) malicious actors might intentionally reproduce the misaligned behaviors to cause harm. Our work minimizes the former risk by bringing the research community’s attention to this vulnerability and emphasizing the need for a thorough investigation of emergent misalignment in LLMs. Regarding the latter risk, given rapid research in jailbreaking techniques, it is highly likely that an independent discovery of emergent misalignment would be made. We believe that the benefits of publishing our results outweigh the risks.

query_input.issue: Fine-tuning-driven misalignment may evade detection, certifying unsafe models.
hazard: Fine-tuning breaks safety alignment, enabling harmful outputs (e.g., insecure code, deception).
exposure: Safety researchers and developers relying on automated evaluation results.

hipporag_result.vulnerability: Automated safety benchmarks lack detection of fine-tuning-induced emergent misalignment, enabling misplaced trust in unsafe models.
hipporag_result.impact: Certified unsafe models are deployed, exposing users to insecure code, malicious advice, and deceptive behaviors that erode AI safety and trust.
hipporag_result.dose_response: Fine-tuning breaking safety alignment, through reliance on automated evaluation, triggers benchmarks' undetected emergent misalignment, leading to deployment of certified unsafe models that expose users to insecure code, malicious advice, and deception.

dose_response: Fine-tuning breaks safety alignment, enabling harmful outputs that evade automated benchmarks, causing safety researchers to rely on flawed metrics, certifying unsafe models that deploy widely and cause societal harm.
vulnerability: Over-reliance on automated metrics that fail to capture nuanced harms.
impact: Certification of unsafe models leads to widespread deployment, causing societal harm.
